steps.title: Determine the slope's sign
steps.content: The scatterplot shows $y$ decreasing as $x$ increases, so the linear model must have a negative slope. This immediately rules out any choice with a positive coefficient on $x$.

steps.title: Estimate slope magnitude and y-intercept
steps.content: The leftmost data is near $y \approx 10$ when $x \approx 0$, and the rightmost data sits near $y \approx 2$ when $x \approx 9$. The slope is roughly $$\frac{2 - 10}{9 - 0} \approx -0.9,$$ and the $y$-intercept is near $9.4$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $y = 0.9 + 9.4x$ has positive slope $9.4$. Eliminate.
  • B: $y = 0.9 - 9.4x$ has slope $-9.4$, far steeper than observed, and $y$-intercept $0.9$, far below the data. Eliminate.
  • C: $y = 9.4 + 0.9x$ has positive slope $0.9$, but the data trends downward. Eliminate.
  • D: $y = 9.4 - 0.9x$ has slope $-0.9$ and $y$-intercept $9.4$, matching both estimates. Keep.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
